cases.title: The Black Orchid Room
cases.brief: Mara Voss was found dead after a private meeting in her hotel room. The door was locked from the inside, the hallway camera skipped one minute, and someone left the room with the victim's keycard.
cases.scene: The room contains a half-finished glass of red wine, crushed orchid petals, a forced-open desk drawer, and a missing envelope from the safe. A hotel staff log shows one keycard use that does not match the victim's normal routine.

suspects.name: Nina Vale
suspects.profile: Mara's assistant who handled travel, payments, and private meetings. She knows the room routine better than the hotel staff.
suspects.alibi: She says she was downstairs printing invoices when the murder happened, but no one remembers seeing her at the printer long enough to prove it.
suspects.motive: Inheritance. If Mara changed her will, Nina stood to lose everything she expected to receive.
suspects.relationship: Trusted employee with full access to schedules, accounts, and hotel arrangements.

suspects.name: Adrian Kells
suspects.profile: Co-owner of Mara's art deals. He argues in public but smiles in private.
suspects.alibi: He claims he was in the lobby on a call with a collector.
suspects.motive: Blackmail. Mara had proof he was falsifying provenance documents.
suspects.relationship: Long-time business partner who benefited from Mara's silence.

suspects.name: Theo Grant
suspects.profile: Night clerk who knows which guests ask for privacy and which ones ask for loopholes.
suspects.alibi: He says he never left the front desk.
suspects.motive: Cover-up. He may have helped someone bypass hotel policy.
suspects.relationship: Only staff member with access to keycard overrides.

suspects.name: Dr. Celia Rowe
suspects.profile: Mara's physician and long-time friend. She prescribed a mild sedative the week before the murder.
suspects.alibi: She says she was on a video call with a patient.
suspects.motive: Jealousy. Mara was planning to expose something she had confided in Celia.
suspects.relationship: Medical access and knowledge of Mara's prescriptions.

evidence.title: Crime Scene Overview
evidence.summary: A wide room photo shows the wine glass, the orchid petals, and the open desk drawer.
evidence.details: The photo places the glass near the bed, the petals by the desk, and a faint drag mark leading toward the bathroom.

evidence.title: Toxicology Report
evidence.summary: Digitalis traces were found in the wine glass residue.
evidence.details: The medical examiner notes a fast onset of cardiac failure after ingestion. Digitalis is not part of Mara's normal prescriptions.

evidence.title: Hallway Camera Clip
evidence.summary: The footage skips from 8:11 PM to 8:12 PM.
evidence.details: During the missing minute, the corridor is briefly blank. The staff log shows one keycard override at exactly 8:11 PM.

evidence.title: Voicemail Transcript
evidence.summary: Mara's voicemail accuses Nina of touching the accounts.
evidence.details: Transcript: 'Nina, if you moved the transfer again, we're done. I know what you were hiding in the ledger.'

evidence.title: Keycard Log
evidence.summary: A staff key was used to open Room 214 after the victim's final phone call.
evidence.details: The access record belongs to the hotel desk override and appears only once in the night, which narrows the suspect with hotel access.

evidence.title: Desk Drawer Inventory
evidence.summary: A signed transfer envelope is missing from the desk drawer.
evidence.details: The drawer was forced after the victim died, suggesting someone searched for a document rather than random valuables.

cases.title: Dead Air at Studio Nine
cases.brief: Jon Mercer was found dead inside the broadcast booth minutes before the end of a live segment. The mic was still open when the feed cut to silence.
cases.scene: The booth has a displaced chair, a cut microphone cable, and a mixer that was adjusted after the last planned ad break. Security footage shows a short gap in the backstage corridor.

suspects.name: Owen Pike
suspects.profile: He manages levels, filters, and booth access. He can make a recording tell almost any story.
suspects.alibi: He says he was checking the room mix in the control booth.
suspects.motive: Blackmail. Jon had a recording that could ruin him.
suspects.relationship: Only person with unrestricted access to the mic chain and booth routing.

suspects.name: Lena Hart
suspects.profile: She keeps the show on air and knows exactly which guests were invited for ratings.
suspects.alibi: She says she was on a phone call with the sponsor.
suspects.motive: Career sabotage. Jon threatened to remove her from the show.
suspects.relationship: The person most likely to benefit from a public scandal.

suspects.name: Sera Knox
suspects.profile: A performer who was humiliated by Jon during a prior interview.
suspects.alibi: She says she was warming up in a dressing room.
suspects.motive: Revenge. Jon insulted her and leaked part of her personal message.
suspects.relationship: Recent guest who had a reason to confront Jon, but not the booth access.

suspects.name: Marcus Vale
suspects.profile: Responsible for studio doors and corridor cameras.
suspects.alibi: He says the monitor had a dead spot and he stayed at his post.
suspects.motive: Cover-up. He may have let someone inside the booth without logging it.
suspects.relationship: The only guard on the floor when the feed cut.

evidence.title: Dead Air Recording
evidence.summary: The live feed cuts, then a scrape and a thump are heard before the silence.
evidence.details: A quiet voice in the background says, 'I told you not to keep that file.' The microphone peaks at the exact moment the cable is cut.

evidence.title: Backstage Corridor Clip
evidence.summary: A camera gap appears just as someone enters the hall with a coil of cable.
evidence.details: The footage resumes with the same person leaving too quickly to be a normal equipment check. The timing matches the broadcast interruption.

evidence.title: Fiber Analysis
evidence.summary: Fibers on the victim's neck match the studio's spare microphone cable.
evidence.details: The cable cut pattern suggests it was used as a weapon before being severed from the booth setup.

evidence.title: Deleted Message Thread
evidence.summary: A hidden chat log references a recording the victim refused to hand over.
evidence.details: The thread ends with: 'If it airs, we're both done.' The sender name is partially obscured but the timing points to the sound engineer.

evidence.title: Mixer Position Photo
evidence.summary: One fader is slid past the usual live setting, causing a brief spike in the feed.
evidence.details: The photo shows manual adjustment after the show schedule was locked. This makes the audio cut feel staged rather than accidental.

evidence.title: Studio Access Log
evidence.summary: Owen Pike accessed Studio 9 two minutes before dead air.
evidence.details: The log is signed with his badge and the corridor camera gap lines up with the same window.

cases.title: Frozen Shift
cases.location: Cold storage bay, Northline Processing Plant
cases.brief: Elias Wren was discovered inside a locked freezer during an overnight inventory shift. The emergency release had been tampered with, but the death occurred before the freeze could be blamed.
cases.scene: The bay floor shows a forklift track, a damaged pallet seal, and a maintenance panel left open. Temperature logs indicate the freezer door was opened and closed twice within a short window.

suspects.name: Dana Holt
suspects.profile: Runs the overnight shift and controls dispatch approvals.
suspects.alibi: She says she was in the office reviewing inventory variance reports.
suspects.motive: Embezzlement. Elias was about to report missing stock tied to her approvals.
suspects.relationship: Direct manager with authority over the freezer bay schedule.

suspects.name: Priya Sen
suspects.profile: Keeps the floor moving and notices when someone changes a route.
suspects.alibi: She says she was checking a shipment at the dock.
suspects.motive: Cover-up. She may have hidden a discrepancy in the manifest.
suspects.relationship: The person most familiar with the freezer alarm resets.

suspects.name: Gabriel Ford
suspects.profile: Delivered the last pallet before the incident and argued with Elias earlier that night.
suspects.alibi: He says he left the plant after unloading.
suspects.motive: Revenge. Elias blamed him for a lost shipment.
suspects.relationship: Had a loud public dispute with the victim two days before the murder.

suspects.name: Tessa Bell
suspects.profile: Tracks invoices, overages, and the paper trail that everyone else tries to avoid.
suspects.alibi: She says she was reconciling the books from home.
suspects.motive: Personal grudge. Elias rejected her audit request and threatened to expose her work.
suspects.relationship: Knows which numbers do and do not fit together.

evidence.title: Freezer Bay Scene Photo
evidence.summary: The victim's body is near the emergency release panel, with a forklift path across the floor.
evidence.details: The photo suggests movement after injury rather than a simple accidental lock-in.

evidence.title: Inventory Audit Sheet
evidence.summary: Repeated stock losses were approved under Dana Holt's shift credentials.
evidence.details: The audit shows missing product that was never reported to the plant owner. Elias had marked it for escalation.

evidence.title: Dispatch Call Recording
evidence.summary: A call records Dana telling someone to 'keep Elias out of the bay until the count is clean.'
evidence.details: The recording includes the freezer alarm being shut off manually a few minutes later.

evidence.title: Forklift Security Clip
evidence.summary: A forklift passes the freezer bay after the shift change, then reverses without unloading.
evidence.details: The clip shows a person in a supervisor jacket using the maintenance access door. The motion is too careful to be an accident.

evidence.title: Maintenance Panel Notes
evidence.summary: Someone disabled the freezer alarm circuit and reclosed the panel.
evidence.details: The note is unsigned, but the handoff time matches the exact gap in the temperature log.

evidence.title: Temperature Log
evidence.summary: The freezer was opened twice within twelve minutes.
evidence.details: The first opening likely allowed the killer inside the bay. The second likely helped stage the final scene.
